sections.paragraph:
ἐπιφώσκω (Poeta De Herb. 25; pap; mid. Job 41:10 v.l.) to grow towards or become daylight, shine forth, dawn, break, perh. draw on (PLond I, 130, 39 p. 134 [I/II a.d.]; PGrenf II, 112, 15 τῇ ἐπιφωσκούσῃ κυριακῇ; Ps.-Clem., Hom. 3, 1 p. 36, 23 Lag.) σάββατον ἐπέφωσκεν the Sabbath dawned or drew on Lk 23:54; cp. GPt 2:5. ἐπιφώσκοντος τοῦ σαββάτου 9:34; cp. vs. 35. τῇ ἐπιφωσκούσῃ εἰς μίαν σαββάτων as the first day of the week dawned or drew near Mt 28:1. See GMoore, JAOS 26, 1905, 323–29; CTurner, JTS 14, 1913, 188–90; FBurkitt, ibid. 538–46; PGardner-Smith, ibid. 27, 1926, 179–81; MBlack, An Aramaic Approach3, ’67, 136–38. S. also on ἐπιφαύσκω.—M-M.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

perh. perh. = perhaps

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PGrenf II PGrenf II = BGrenfell/AHunt, New Classical Fragments—Lists 4

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

JAOS JAOS = Journal of the American Oriental Society—List 6

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 386
